programme.title: Arctic Animals
programme.desc: In this episode, we'll feature animals that live in the freezing arctic. Learn how the Polar Bear has developed special adaptations that help it survive in the most unforgiving climate imaginable. And why the Wolverine is able to hunt so efficiently in the snow.

programme.title: Man's Best Friend
programme.desc: In this episode, we examine the science behind man's best friend - the dog. Find out what makes a greyhound so fast. See how a bloodhound's nose really works, and the reason that border collies are so intelligent is revealed.

programme.title: Natural Disasters
programme.desc: This week we see how science helps us prepare for natural disasters. Philippe heads to the University of Miami to see their incredible SUSTAIN lab that can simulate a category 5 hurricane. Then it's off to the mountains of Alberta to learn about avalanche safety. From there's we head to South Carolina to follow researchers that study floods. And finally at the University of Reno a "shake table" helps with designing buildings that can hold up during earthquakes.

programme.title: Taking Flight
programme.desc: This week we take flight within the animal kingdom. Philippe joins a researcher to go into Congaree National Park in South Carolina to catch and tag local bats. We then look at the flight patterns and tendencies of wood storks. And finally from caterpillar to butterfly we examine the transformation from crawling to flying.

programme.title: Primarily Primates
programme.desc: In this episode we meet human's closest relatives - primates. See what makes a chimpanzee one of the most intelligent primates. Find out why the spider monkey is such an amazing climber and feel the power of the howler monkey's howl.

programme.title: Surf and Turf
programme.desc: In this episode we'll find out how sharks use electro-sensors to detect their prey. Also, discover the skunk's stinky defense mechanism. And domestic versus wild - see how the striped zebra matches up against its cousin the horse.

programme.title: Good To Know

programme.title: Sikh Temple Shooting
programme.desc: Scripps News revisits the the Sikh Temple in Wisconsin in the community of Oak Creek, a site of a tragic shooting on August 5, 2012, where seven worshipers lost their lives to an act fueled by hate and extremism. More than a decade later, we delve into the community's journey of healing, resilience, and the power of forgiveness.

programme.title: Afghanistan Withdrawal
programme.desc: Scripps News explores the lives impacted by the U.S. troop withdrawal from Afghanistan in 2021. Critics say the government's decision lacked foresight, ultimately forcing the U.S. to take on tens of thousands of refugees. More than two years later, those refugees are still struggling to get back on their feet.

programme.title: Dead Silence
programme.desc: In Michigan an ex-cop is so obsessed with his lover he's prepared to do anything for her, and in Ontario, Canada sex becomes a powerful weapon as a marriage falls apart

programme.title: Flight Of Fancy
programme.desc: In Newtown, CT a flight attendant vanishes into the night after returning home, while in Virginia a trip to an ATM machine ends in mystery and murder.

programme.title: Deception
programme.desc: In Virginia Beach, VA, there is a twisted plot involving murder to try and secure a pay out from the U.S. Navy, while in Grovetown, GA, a mysterious burglar seems to want two women neighbors dead.

programme.title: What Lurks Within
programme.desc: In a suburb of Nashville, TN, a wife's secret lover sets up home in her closet, while in Cave Creek, Arizona, a couple look like they are out for a drive, but only one of them is alive.

programme.title: Operation Luau: Trapping A Dirty Cop
programme.desc: Joanne Takasato has no experience in uniform or on the streets of Honolulu. Nevertheless, she works the streets as a small time druggie, making buys before she's assigned the task of taking down a dirty cop. She's intimidated and threatened by the cop, and even her supervisor is jealous of her success on the streets.

programme.title: Operation Cockfight: Underworld Gambling
programme.desc: Stan Sales of the Honolulu PD enters the world of cockfighting, a popular entertainment on the island. Sales and his partners go undercover to videotape and document a local promotor who controls the cockfights and takes a piece of all the action. They eventually arrest the promoter and shut down his illegal operation for good.

programme.title: Operation Bank Robbery: Breaking The Bank
programme.desc: Ken Dye goes undercover for the St. Louis County Sheriff's Department to purchase illegal weapons and hears about a planned bank heist. He is hired to help the would-be bank robbers stick up a local bank. But Dye discovers that the mastermind plans to enact vengeance for being refused a loan, and kill the bank president. Dye plays along to gather evidence against the criminals and arrest them before they go rogue and pull the job without him.

programme.title: Operation Canned Tomatoes: Italian Mafia
programme.desc: FBI agent Ron Moretti enters the dangerous world of the Italian mafia, while investigating the supply of heroin into the US. Moretti devises a plan to have the mafia ship their white powder inside canned tomatoes, but the FDA blocks the plan because of their culinary concerns. During the two-year long operation, Moretti becomes the shipping point of Italian heroin, while dodging international governments before taking the traffickers down.

programme.title: Today As It Happened
programme.desc: What happened today? Find out as Scripps News gets you up to speed on the day's events that everyone is talking about.

programme.title: Homicide In The High Desert
programme.desc: When the soon-to-be ex-wife of a prominent investment banker dies inside her Arizona home, it initially appears as though she suffered an accidental fall. Yet, through the dogged persistence of family and friends, and after months of intense investigation, law enforcement discovers the murder was no accident, but an act of rage carried out by a desperate man with hundreds of thousands to gain.

programme.title: Bonded In Murder
programme.desc: A street preacher and his teenage protege run an Internet scam to rob unsuspecting men, steal their identities, then dump their dead bodies in the woods. The scheme works multiple times until one of the marks escapes and brings law enforcement right to their doorstep.

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: You can run. You can hide. But chances are, they’re still gonna find you. These are some of the wildest and most bizarre police captures on record.

programme.title: WGAL In Focus
programme.desc: In this episode of In Focus, Forgotten Friend Reptile Sanctuary in Lancaster County takes in reptiles in need of help. The sanctuary also educates adults and children about snakes, turtles, and tortoises. Today we’ll see some reptiles, learn about conservation efforts, and the dangers of buying reptiles over the Internet.

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: A duo pulls a Bonnie and Clyde in a truck stolen from a dealership, and a pair takes money from a monastery. These shocking stories of couples in crime have to be witnessed to be believed.

programme.title: Ed & Day in The 'Burgh
programme.desc: Ed and Day learn that in Homestead, looks can be deceiving. The guys throw axes in a bank, zip line in a church, and end the day with a beer at Duke's.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: When homesick college student Miya Marcano books a flight to south Florida for the weekend, her family is thrilled. But when the plane lands, Miya is nowhere to be found.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: In this battle, it’s a sizzling pork showdown. Chef Travis Chasteen of RusTeak in Thornton Park faces off with Chef John Collazo of Bad A's Sandwiches in the Milk District. It’s Pork Belly vs. Tomahawk on this episode of Blind Kitchen.

programme.title: Absolutely Albuquerque
programme.desc: Old classics get a fun twist. Amanda hits the town with expert mixologist Kate Gerwin, who introduces her to a custom sneaker shop, a new pizza place with some unexpected ingredients, and the city’s unique lowrider scene.

programme.title: Local Love
programme.desc: Des Moines single Riley is ready for a second chance at love. Will a decor hunt spark creative chemistry, a pickleball match serve up a familiar connection or will axe-throwing hit the bullseye?

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
programme.desc: These criminals may not have blood on their hands, but they still leave despair in their wake. From shady non-profits to fraudulent contractors, to wide-spread postal theft, here are some of the most devastating white-collar crimes to be attempted across the country.

programme.title: Finding Adventure
programme.desc: Anthony Carignan is a bookish 26-year-old who has never felt at ease in the outdoors. Kinga has come to Maine to help Anthony connect with nature and live out his dream of becoming an authentic Mainer.

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Embark on an out-of-this-world journey to Roswell, New Mexico! Unveil intriguing alien mysteries, order from a McDonald's with a secret menu and stay the night in a Cold War missile bunker. Don't miss this extraordinary adventure!

programme.title: Eat Play Stay
programme.desc: Go climbing, sample sizzling hot dogs and cold beer, throw a few axes and explore the rich history on the border of PA & Ohio.

programme.title: Ed & Day in The 'Burgh
programme.desc: Ed and Day venture to Verona and explore “the spot that’s more than a dot.” The guys play pickleball, dine on BBQ, get creative with splatter paint, and finish the day with a brew.

programme.title: Boston Rob Does Beantown
programme.desc: From fencing, to ninja warrior training, to boxing and Tai Chi, Rob discovers an inner strength he never knew he had…while getting kicked in the keister.

programme.title: Blind Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Michael Ferrazzani, creator of Ya Mamma’s sauce, challenges the North End born Italian stallion, Chef Kenny Palazzolo, to a true neighborhood duel. Can Michael’s Grandma inspired dish bring down the chef who lives, laughs and cooks Italian?

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: Three brutal cold cases remain unsolved for decades until advances in forensic technology bring investigators closer than ever to finding answers for the victims’ families and friends.

programme.title: The Spark
programme.desc: A Roxbury iconoclast whose work defined hip-hop’s golden age, Rob ‘ProBlak’ Gibbs made his name with murals, but became a legend by giving a voice to the unheard.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Ted wants to live to 100 with his beloved wife beside him. But his oral health threatens his future. Kristy’s a FIXT staffer desperate to smile big on her wedding day. Will love help them face their fears?

programme.title: Chaos & Kindness
programme.desc: Justin sends Ryan on a thrilling musical journey in Nashville with just $20 and drumsticks to earn his ticket back home. On the scorching sidewalks of Music City, Ryan is humbled and uplifted by the generosity of those who have the least to give.

programme.title: Stitch
programme.desc: The best stories can transform us into magical worlds. They can also provide comfort or challenge us to learn new perspectives. This episode highlights authors, librarians and book lovers alike who make the world a better place.

programme.title: Show Me Milwaukee
programme.desc: Join Greg and Maryam Durani as they explore Milwaukee from the eyes of their newest neighbors. Greg volunteers with kids at the library, hears powerful stories, and bakes specialty bread from Maryam's homeland. Don't miss this inspiring episode!

programme.title: Completed Episodes
programme.desc: Frank Nicotero quizzes people in New Orleans about a starchy substance in a bathtub, animal mysteries and robots that do the darndest things.

programme.title: Ballin' in Baltimore
programme.desc: Angel captures a different angle on Baltimore with activist and photojournalist, Devin Allen. They explore street photography, pop wheelies with a cutting-edge community dirt biking program and enjoy Charm City's most decadent treats.

programme.title: The Middle Child
programme.desc: In the 1980s, a serial killer buried a woman and three girls in the woods of New Hampshire. One of those girls is still unidentified. WMUR joins the investigators who promise they won’t give up until her name is restored.

programme.title: Show Me Milwaukee
programme.desc: Local radio personality, Greg Adams, wants to shine a light on all parts of his beloved city and prove that Milwaukee has a lot more to offer than people might expect. In each episode, Greg is joined by a notable personality from the community who shows him their favorite spots and the best ways to experience them.

programme.title: Restaurants on the Radar
programme.desc: Brendan O'Connor has been tracking down and writing about hidden gem restaurants in Orlando for over a decade. Now you get to come along with him on a foodie tour of The City Beautiful to see what makes these places so special and a must try

programme.title: Arctic Animals
programme.desc: In this episode, we'll feature animals that live in the freezing arctic. Learn how the Polar Bear has developed special adaptations that help it survive in the most unforgiving climate imaginable. And why the Wolverine is able to hunt so efficiently in the snow.

programme.title: Man's Best Friend
programme.desc: In this episode, we examine the science behind man's best friend - the dog. Find out what makes a greyhound so fast. See how a bloodhound's nose really works, and the reason that border collies are so intelligent is revealed.

programme.title: Natural Disasters
programme.desc: This week we see how science helps us prepare for natural disasters. Philippe heads to the University of Miami to see their incredible SUSTAIN lab that can simulate a category 5 hurricane. Then it's off to the mountains of Alberta to learn about avalanche safety. From there's we head to South Carolina to follow researchers that study floods. And finally at the University of Reno a "shake table" helps with designing buildings that can hold up during earthquakes.

programme.title: Taking Flight
programme.desc: This week we take flight within the animal kingdom. Philippe joins a researcher to go into Congaree National Park in South Carolina to catch and tag local bats. We then look at the flight patterns and tendencies of wood storks. And finally from caterpillar to butterfly we examine the transformation from crawling to flying.

programme.title: Primarily Primates
programme.desc: In this episode we meet human's closest relatives - primates. See what makes a chimpanzee one of the most intelligent primates. Find out why the spider monkey is such an amazing climber and feel the power of the howler monkey's howl.

programme.title: Surf and Turf
programme.desc: In this episode we'll find out how sharks use electro-sensors to detect their prey. Also, discover the skunk's stinky defense mechanism. And domestic versus wild - see how the striped zebra matches up against its cousin the horse.

programme.title: Sikh Temple Shooting
programme.desc: Scripps News revisits the the Sikh Temple in Wisconsin in the community of Oak Creek, a site of a tragic shooting on August 5, 2012, where seven worshipers lost their lives to an act fueled by hate and extremism. More than a decade later, we delve into the community's journey of healing, resilience, and the power of forgiveness.

programme.title: Afghanistan Withdrawal
programme.desc: Scripps News explores the lives impacted by the U.S. troop withdrawal from Afghanistan in 2021. Critics say the government's decision lacked foresight, ultimately forcing the U.S. to take on tens of thousands of refugees. More than two years later, those refugees are still struggling to get back on their feet.

programme.title: What Lurks Within
programme.desc: In a suburb of Nashville, TN, a wife's secret lover sets up home in her closet, while in Cave Creek, Arizona, a couple look like they are out for a drive, but only one of them is alive.

programme.title: Operation Luau: Trapping A Dirty Cop
programme.desc: Joanne Takasato has no experience in uniform or on the streets of Honolulu. Nevertheless, she works the streets as a small time druggie, making buys before she's assigned the task of taking down a dirty cop. She's intimidated and threatened by the cop, and even her supervisor is jealous of her success on the streets.

programme.title: Operation Cockfight: Underworld Gambling
programme.desc: Stan Sales of the Honolulu PD enters the world of cockfighting, a popular entertainment on the island. Sales and his partners go undercover to videotape and document a local promotor who controls the cockfights and takes a piece of all the action. They eventually arrest the promoter and shut down his illegal operation for good.

programme.title: Operation Bank Robbery: Breaking The Bank
programme.desc: Ken Dye goes undercover for the St. Louis County Sheriff's Department to purchase illegal weapons and hears about a planned bank heist. He is hired to help the would-be bank robbers stick up a local bank. But Dye discovers that the mastermind plans to enact vengeance for being refused a loan, and kill the bank president. Dye plays along to gather evidence against the criminals and arrest them before they go rogue and pull the job without him.

programme.title: Operation Canned Tomatoes: Italian Mafia
programme.desc: FBI agent Ron Moretti enters the dangerous world of the Italian mafia, while investigating the supply of heroin into the US. Moretti devises a plan to have the mafia ship their white powder inside canned tomatoes, but the FDA blocks the plan because of their culinary concerns. During the two-year long operation, Moretti becomes the shipping point of Italian heroin, while dodging international governments before taking the traffickers down.

programme.title: Arctic Animals
programme.desc: In this episode, we'll feature animals that live in the freezing arctic. Learn how the Polar Bear has developed special adaptations that help it survive in the most unforgiving climate imaginable. And why the Wolverine is able to hunt so efficiently in the snow.

programme.title: Man's Best Friend
programme.desc: In this episode, we examine the science behind man's best friend - the dog. Find out what makes a greyhound so fast. See how a bloodhound's nose really works, and the reason that border collies are so intelligent is revealed.

programme.title: Natural Disasters
programme.desc: This week we see how science helps us prepare for natural disasters. Philippe heads to the University of Miami to see their incredible SUSTAIN lab that can simulate a category 5 hurricane. Then it's off to the mountains of Alberta to learn about avalanche safety. From there's we head to South Carolina to follow researchers that study floods. And finally at the University of Reno a "shake table" helps with designing buildings that can hold up during earthquakes.

programme.title: Taking Flight
programme.desc: This week we take flight within the animal kingdom. Philippe joins a researcher to go into Congaree National Park in South Carolina to catch and tag local bats. We then look at the flight patterns and tendencies of wood storks. And finally from caterpillar to butterfly we examine the transformation from crawling to flying.

programme.title: Primarily Primates
programme.desc: In this episode we meet human's closest relatives - primates. See what makes a chimpanzee one of the most intelligent primates. Find out why the spider monkey is such an amazing climber and feel the power of the howler monkey's howl.

programme.title: Surf and Turf
programme.desc: In this episode we'll find out how sharks use electro-sensors to detect their prey. Also, discover the skunk's stinky defense mechanism. And domestic versus wild - see how the striped zebra matches up against its cousin the horse.
